codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers all feature requirements: Benchmark SQLAlchemy model with correct fields, Alembic migration, BenchmarkCreate/BenchmarkRead Pydantic schemas with min/max/duplicate URL validation, POST/GET list/GET single API routes returning correct status codes, benchmark orchestrator that spawns audits per URL and aggregates trust_scores. 22 comprehensive tests pass. Code follows existing patterns (matches audits router structure). Several non-blocking issues identified.

issues.description: Stale object reference: After the 'with self.session_factory() as db' block closes (line 101), benchmark.audit_ids is accessed on line 108 outside the session scope. This works because SQLAlchemy eagerly loads JSON columns (not lazy relationships), but accessing a detached ORM object is a code smell and could break if session behavior changes.

Benchmark status is always set to 'completed' regardless of whether all_completed is True or False (both branches of the if/else on lines 125-129 set status='completed'). The 'failed' status is only reachable via _handle_benchmark_failure for unhandled exceptions. If individual audits fail, the benchmark still shows 'completed'. This is technically documented in the comment, but may confuse consumers expecting 'failed' for partial failures.
BenchmarkListResponse class is defined but never used anywhere. The list endpoint returns list[BenchmarkRead] directly. This is dead code that should be removed.

'import time' is placed inside the _wait_for_audit_and_get_score method body rather than at module top level. Similarly 'import logging' is inside _handle_benchmark_failure. PEP 8 convention is to import at the top of the file.

f-string used in logger.critical() call: logger.critical(f"Failed to update benchmark {benchmark_id} status to failed"). Should use logger.critical("...", benchmark_id=benchmark_id) or % formatting to support structured logging. The project AGENTS.md says to use structlog.
Migration file uses autogenerated hash naming (6e81669bfdad_add_benchmarks_table.py) but AGENTS.md specifies convention: YYYYMMDD_NNNN_description.py. Existing migrations use 20260314_NNNN prefix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Alembic migration was named using autogenerate hash (6e81669bfdad_) instead of the YYYYMMDD_NNNN_ naming convention documented in AGENTS.md. The skill file instructs workers to run 'alembic revision --autogenerate' which produces hash names by default. Either the convention documentation should be updated to reflect that autogenerate creates hash names, or the skill should instruct workers to rename the file after autogeneration, or alembic.ini/env.py should be configured with a custom file_template.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md says 'Alembic migrations for all schema changes (naming: YYYYMMDD_NNNN_description.py)'. SKILL.md says 'alembic revision --autogenerate -m description'. Result: 6e81669bfdad_add_benchmarks_table.py

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library file states '3 tables: audits, findings, audit_events' under the Database section. Now there's a 4th table 'benchmarks'. This should be updated to avoid misleading future workers about the database schema.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md line 'Database: 3 tables: audits, findings, audit_events' is now stale after adding benchmarks table.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skipped mypy step from the backend-dev skill procedure (step 5). The handoff verification commands show ruff check was run but not mypy. This may have been justified by the known 95 pre-existing mypy errors noted in mission AGENTS.md, but the skill procedure doesn't acknowledge this exception. Skill should note: 'If AGENTS.md documents known mypy errors, run mypy but only verify no NEW errors were introduced.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification.commandsRun has no mypy command. SKILL.md step 5: 'Run mypy: cd backend && .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m mypy app/ --ignore-missing-imports'. Mission AGENTS.md: 'Backend mypy errors (95 errors): Pre-existing type errors... Do NOT let these block scrutiny validation.'

summary: PASS. The benchmark-backend-model-and-api feature is fully implemented: Benchmark model with all required fields, Alembic migration, BenchmarkCreate/BenchmarkRead schemas with URL count and uniqueness validation, POST (201)/GET list/GET single API routes, benchmark orchestrator that spawns audits per URL and aggregates trust_scores. 22 tests cover happy paths, validation edge cases, and status lifecycle. All 303 tests pass. No blocking issues found. Non-blocking issues: dead BenchmarkListResponse schema, stale ORM object reference in orchestrator, both branches of audit completion always set status='completed', inline imports, f-string in logger, migration naming convention mismatch.
